name: obsidian-cli
description: Obsidian CLI control — search/read/create notes, daily notes, tags, tasks, run commands & JavaScript, reload plugins, all against a running Obsidian instance.

prerequisites.name: obsidian-app
prerequisites.install_hint: Install Obsidian 1.9+ from https://obsidian.md, then enable the CLI in Settings -> General and register it (one-time GUI step). On Windows this adds a console redirector (Obsidian.com) next to Obsidian.exe. IMPORTANT: the installer .exe shell must be >= 1.11.7 for the redirector to exist -- the app package auto-updates but the shell does NOT, so an old shell makes every 'obsidian' command open the GUI window instead. If that happens, rerun the current installer, then re-Register.
prerequisites.note: The CLI is built into the Obsidian desktop app; there is no separate package. Obsidian must be RUNNING for any command to work.

install.notes: Nothing to download. The CLI ships inside the Obsidian desktop app. Enable it once via Settings -> General -> 'Obsidian CLI' and follow the on-screen Register instructions (macOS: symlink in /usr/local/bin; Linux: ~/.local/bin/obsidian; Windows: console redirector Obsidian.com beside Obsidian.exe, requires installer .exe >= 1.11.7). After registering, restart your shell so PATH/redirector resolves.

integration_test.description: Obsidian must be running with a vault open.

post_install_hints: Obsidian CLI is built into the app — enable it in Settings -> General, then click Register (one-time, requires GUI).
Obsidian must be running for commands to succeed.
Target a specific vault with the vault="VaultName" parameter; add format=json for machine-readable output.
